clientContext: Michael's own software/web dev agency seeking to remove client-acquisition bottleneck via automated B2B lead discovery and qualification.

leadDiscoveryEngine.description: Modular search pipeline targeting small-to-medium businesses in Minnesota for software/web development opportunities.
specifics: Search via Google/Serper API and localized directory scraping
Geographic scope: Twin Cities metro (Minneapolis, St. Paul, Bloomington, Edina) + Greater Minnesota (Duluth, Rochester, St. Cloud, Mankato)

Target industries: Entertainment & Leisure, Professional Services, Home & Commercial Services, Healthcare & Wellness, Retail & Hospitality, Manufacturing & Logistics (with specific examples: bowling alleys, ad agencies, HVAC, dental, breweries, machine shops)

webgpuAiTechnicalAudit.description: Local in-browser AI inference for evaluating business websites without external API calls.
specifics: Scrape target business homepage and capture full-page screenshot

HARD REQUIREMENT: STRICTLY use WebGPU for local AI inference (WebLLM or Transformers.js in headless browser with --enable-unsafe-webgpu --enable-features=Vulkan flags, OR Python-native WebGPU engine like MLC LLM)
Score website on 1-10 scale: Modernity (UI/UX), Mobile Responsiveness, Functionality
Flag outdated tech stacks (legacy WordPress, missing HTTPS, non-responsive layouts)
Provide audit notes with breakdown of sub-scores and rationale

contactEnrichmentPipeline.description: Extract and cross-reference business decision-maker contact information.
specifics: Extract from website: emails, phone numbers, contact forms
Cross-reference company name via professional data APIs (Proxycurl, Apollo) to identify key decision-makers
Target titles: CEO, Owner, Founder, VP of Marketing, CTO, VP Sales
Avoid aggressive LinkedIn bot scraping (use API-based lookups only)

frontendDashboardCRM.description: Interactive data-table CRM for managing and tracking B2B sales pipeline.
features: Interactive spreadsheet/data-table with columns: Company Name, Industry, Location, Tech Audit Score, Outdated Stack (Y/N), Key Decision Maker, Outreach Status
Sortable and filterable by: industry, location, audit score, outdated-stack status, outreach status
Editable outreach status with enum: New, Contacted, Ignored, In Progress
Drill-down modal per lead displaying: captured homepage screenshot, specific AI-generated audit notes (with sub-score breakdown), decision-maker profile card (name, title, seniority)
Bulk status edits and lead-state persistence
Summary KPIs: total leads, by-industry breakdown, by-status breakdown, average audit score
frontendDashboardCRM.persistence: SQLite (server-backed Flask blueprint /clientfinder/ recommended for authenticity at richness 10, seeded with realistic Twin Cities/Greater Minnesota business data)

heroFeature.name: Interactive Lead CRM Table + Drill-Down Modal
heroFeature.description: Sortable/filterable spreadsheet of B2B leads with one-click drill-down into homepage screenshot, AI audit notes, and decision-maker info. Status is editable and persists locally.
keyCapabilities: Drill-down modal: screenshot + audit sub-scores + decision-maker

supportingFeatures.name: Run Discovery Search Panel
supportingFeatures.description: Demonstrates the lead-discovery modular pipeline with industry + region pickers, animated multi-step flow (Search -> Scrape -> WebGPU Audit -> Enrich) appending new seeded leads to the CRM table.

supportingFeatures.name: In-Browser WebGPU Audit Demo
supportingFeatures.description: Real navigator.gpu capability detection and either Transformers.js (WebGPU backend) running a small local model on captured site text/screenshot in-browser, OR a faithful simulated audit with clearly labeled WebGPU capability check and architecture explainer. Prefers REAL WebGPU inference if feasible within budget.

designDirection.aesthetic: B2B data-tool / sales-intelligence (Apollo/Clay/Crunchbase category): clean, dense, trustworthy SaaS

palette.accent: bright accent for CTAs and score chips

designDirection.typography: modern grotesk UI font with tabular numerals for the data table
designDirection.notes: Subtle Minnesota nod (map/region filter) is fine but keep it professional. NOT michaelwegter.com's dark gallery-wall or mustard/cyan palette.

prototypeVsFull.rationale: Scale scope, number of real working features, and polish to match richness 10. The tool must feel genuinely usable.
realWorkingFeatures: Drill-down modal with real screenshots and audit data
WebGPU audit capability detection and demonstration

sanctionedStub.rationale: Cannot be safely run live in demo (cost, API keys, bot bans, ToS violations)
sanctionedStub.approach: Demonstrate with realistic seeded/mock data + clearly-architected pipeline UI. Seed with real MN business names, locations, industries, and plausible decision-maker info.

seedData.requirement: Seeded with realistic Twin Cities and Greater Minnesota lead data
specifics: Use real city names (Minneapolis, St. Paul, Bloomington, Edina, Duluth, Rochester, St. Cloud, Mankato)
Plausible business names matching target industries
Mix of modern and outdated websites (modernity scores 2-9)

technicalConstraints.webgpuHardRequirement: WebGPU is non-negotiable in narrative and implementation. Even if live model is simulated, demo MUST show real navigator.gpu capability check and explain WebLLM/Transformers.js + Playwright-flags architecture accurately.
technicalConstraints.geographicScope: Twin Cities metro + Greater Minnesota only. No national/nationwide searches.
technicalConstraints.smbFilter: 5-200 employees must be visible in data and UI filtering.
technicalConstraints.smtpVsLocalStorage: SQLite via Flask blueprint /clientfinder/ is the spec and recommended for richness 10. IndexedDB is fallback if budget constraints require it (label clearly as local-SQLite stand-in).

hardGates: Every brief requirement (4 architecture sections) must appear in the demo AND be addressed in the cover letter
Demo builds and loads with zero console errors on first paint
Hero CRM is fully functional: sort, filter, search, bulk-edit, drill-down modal
WebGPU shown (real or simulated with real navigator.gpu capability check)
Seed data uses REAL MN cities and plausible local business names
SMB filter (5-200 employees) visible in UI
Drill-down modal displays: screenshot, audit sub-score breakdown, decision-maker profile
Demo URL resolves locally and live /work-samples/client-finder-1-0 route returns 200 after deploy
Demo link in BOTH cover letter and deck
No em/en dashes anywhere in prose, Michael's voice, GitHub handle mwegter95

auditScoringDimensions.description: UI/UX freshness, design system coherence, modern framework indicators
Viewport adaptation, touch-friendly controls, performance on small screens
Feature completeness, no broken links, contact form validation, site speed
